metadata.title:
IS CITY
CHARTER


VIOLATED?
O'Brien and Tuerke Serve
From the Same


Ward.

pages.raw_text:
Is there a violation of the city
charter in the fact that ]. T.
O,Brien and Carl w. Tuerke, city
commissioners and members Of the
board of public works, are holding
office from the same ward?


1t is charged by those that have
given the matter some attention
that the charter Is being openly VIC
lated and that subterfuge was
used at the time Mr.. Tuerke was
appointed a member of the board OF
works by Mayor Pratt, so as to give
no cause for protest at that time.


Mr.. Tuerke denies that there Is
any violation of the city charter In
his appointment and says that when
he was appointed he had on the adj
vice of the corporation counsel fully
complied with the law. He says the
charter does not cover the case of a
man who is already in office.


Charter amendment 72, section
80, adopted at the city election last
May, provides:


mot more than one of said city
commissioners shall be appointed
from the same ward


When the appointment Of Mr.
Tuerke as city commissioner was
under consideration he moved out
of the First ward, If was announced
by taking rooms at downtown
lodging house Soon after his ap
pointment, Mr.. Tuerke again re
turned to his old home, E2009 Sew
enteenth avenue, where he has con
tinued to live, with the exception Of
the few days that he occupied
rooms downtown during the consid
GRAFTON of his name as a member OF
the board of public works. His fam
lily has resided there continuously


City Commissioner O'Brien re
sides at S2026 Mantle Place, where
he has lived for some time.


In discussing the matter today
YMr. Tuerke said: Yes, / am again
living in the First ward, along with
City Commissioner O'Brien. The
corporation counsel has ruled that
when changed my residence at the
time of my appointment it complied
with the law, as the same refers
only to the appointment of mem,
bers of the board.


1T is nothing new for two mem.
bers of the board of works to be
from the same ward. This has oc.
CURED in at least two instances be.
fore, and no one has said anything
about it."
